title: Fix chat integration bugs: missing await, VS Code type mismatch
description:
Post-integration bug fixes for the chat system:

1. **CRITICAL - Missing `await` in WebSocketServer.ts**: `getMessages()` is async but called without `await` on line 511. This sends a Promise object to plugin clients instead of actual messages, breaking the GET_MESSAGES WebSocket handler.

2. **VS Code Worker type mismatch**: The Worker interface in `moe-vscode/src/types/moe.ts` is missing the `chatCursors?: Record<string, string>` field that exists in the daemon schema (`packages/moe-daemon/src/types/schema.ts` line 169). This causes type inconsistency for schema v5.
definitionOfDone: GET_MESSAGES WebSocket handler awaits getMessages() call and returns actual message array to clients
VS Code Worker interface includes chatCursors field matching daemon schema
VS Code extension compiles without errors

implementationPlan.description: Add missing `await` to the `getMessages()` call in the GET_MESSAGES WebSocket handler. Line 511: change `const messages = this.state.getMessages(channel, {` to `const messages = await this.state.getMessages(channel, {`. The handler is already async so this is safe. Error handling: the existing try/catch in the message handler covers this. Test: verify daemon compiles with `npm run build`.
implementationPlan.note: Added missing await on line 511. Daemon compiles cleanly.

implementationPlan.description: Add `chatCursors?: Record<string, string>` to the VS Code Worker interface in `moe-vscode/src/types/moe.ts` (after line 202, the `teamId` field). This aligns the VS Code types with the daemon schema v5 definition at `packages/moe-daemon/src/types/schema.ts:169`. Test: verify VS Code extension compiles with `npm run compile`.
implementationPlan.note: Added chatCursors optional field to Worker interface after teamId. VS Code extension compiles cleanly.
